en.extract:
The black-bellied malkoha (Phaenicophaeus diardi) is a species of malkoha in the family Cuculidae. It is found in Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, Myanmar, Singapore, and Thailand. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, including mango trees, and subtropical or tropical mangrove forests. It is threatened by habitat loss.

The '''black-bellied malkoha''' ('''''Phaenicophaeus diardi''''') is a species of [[malkoha]] in the family [[Cuculidae]]. It is found in [[Brunei]], [[Indonesia]], [[Malaysia]], [[Myanmar]], [[Singapore]], and [[Thailand]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s, including [[Mangifera indica|mango trees]],<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Iswandaru |first=Dian |last2=Hariyono |last3=Rohman |first3=Fathur |date=2023-05-16 |title=Birding and Avitourism: Potential Analysis of Birds in the Buffer Villages Around Conservation Area |journal=Jurnal Sylva Lestari |volume=11 |issue=2 |pages=247–269 |doi=10.23960/jsl.v11i2.681 |issn=2549-5747|doi-access=free }}</ref> and subtropical or tropical mangrove forests. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].
[[File:Phaenicophaeus diardi 1849.jpg|left|thumb|1849 illustration of black-bellied malkoha from ''Iconographie ornithologique'']]
